info.name: Bifrost Model Catalog Wiring
info.description: End-to-end wiring tests between the management API and the model catalog. Each scenario stands up an isolated, run-namespaced custom provider backed by real OpenAI, mutates its providers/keys, and asserts the catalog read endpoints reflect each mutation. Reads that depend on the asynchronously populated live-model cache poll with exponential backoff. Machine-generated by runners/build-model-catalog-wiring-collection.py — do not hand-edit.

exec: // Build a single run identifier for the whole run so every resource
// created below is namespaced and parallel runs never collide.

item.name: Add provider and key surfaces models
item.description: A fresh provider plus one gated key surfaces that key's allowed model in the catalog.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names));
if (names.indexOf(m) < 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (names.indexOf(m) >= 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' absent but found in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. key model appears in catalog [add-provider-and-key]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
pm.test("03. key model appears in catalog [add-provider-and-key]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });
// Skip the scenario when its required credentials are absent, so a
// developer can run with whatever providers they have configured.

item.name: Updating key model set re-gates the catalog
item.description: Changing a key's allow-list invalidates the stale live entry and re-gates the surfaced models.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names));

try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. initial model gated in [update-key-models]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
pm.test("03. initial model gated in [update-key-models]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

item.name: Disabled key drops models, re-enable restores
item.description: A disabled key is skipped during aggregation; re-enabling it brings its models back.

item.name: Deleting one key leaves sibling models intact
item.description: With two keys gating distinct models, deleting one removes only its models; the sibling's survive.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names));
if (names.indexOf(m) < 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (names.indexOf(m) >= 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' absent but found in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("04. both keys' models present [delete-key-sibling-survives]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
pm.test("04. both keys' models present [delete-key-sibling-survives]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });
if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }

item.name: Deleting provider removes it from the catalog

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }

item.name: Alias resolves to underlying model at inference
item.description: A key alias routes an inference request to the underlying wire model rather than being rejected.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names));
if (names.indexOf(m) < 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (names.indexOf(m) >= 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' absent but found in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. aliased key model present [alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
pm.test("03. aliased key model present [alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });
if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('inference status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()); }
if (!body.choices || body.choices.length === 0) { throw new Error('inference returned no choices'); }
throw new Error('expected resolved model gpt-4o-mini in response.model=' + used);
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });
